steps.title: Spot the core logical link
steps.content: Carter and Kilikoglou argue that production method consistency proves local cultural continuity. Implicitly, they assume that maintaining the same methods indicates the local culture persisted unchanged despite architectural changes.

steps.title: Find what breaks this logic
steps.content:
B shows that these supposedly continuous methods were not unique to Malia but were also used by other Mediterranean cultures. This breaks the chain: method consistency does not necessarily prove local cultural continuity if those same methods were part of a broader Mediterranean technical tradition.

A addresses the obsidian's source, not methods; C suggests less efficient methods, which would support rather than weaken continuity; D indicates that building changes elsewhere also lacked production-method changes, supporting C&K's position.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
